The '''Dazouri''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Changeling/Legends|polymorphs]] that were genetic cousins of the [[Lahsbee/Huhk|Lahsbees]] in a manner analogous to the relationship between [[Human/Legends|Human]] and [[Near-Human]] species. They had two forms, rest and rage. In their rest form, Dazouri were short, hairless [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with large black eyes and vestigial horns on their upper forehead. When a Dazouri was wounded, frightened, threatened, intimidated or infuriated they transformed into their rage form. In rage form they appeared as huge hulking brutes with shaggy black fur, large claws and fangs, and [[Horn/Legends|horns]] on the forehead and cheek. In rest form they were shrewd, calculating creatures that appeared helpless but in their rage form they were homicidal maniacs that attacked everything around them.<ref name="PotG3" /> The Dazouri were [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Gibbela]], where they farmed native crops.<ref name="Hiding" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Dazouri were first mentioned in ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume Three]]''.<ref name="PotG3" /> Years later, a similar species appeared in "[[The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated]]," a two-page comic written by [[Ken Lizzl]] and published in [[Star Wars Tales 19|''Star Wars Tales'' 19]].<ref name="Tales">{{Tales|19|The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated}}</ref> With respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, [[Lucas Licensing]] regarded stories that weren't "completely outrageous or intentionally comic" in the first twenty issues of ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' to be [[Canon|S-canon]] by default, until such time as they were referenced in a non-''Tales'' source, at which point the material became C-canon.<ref name="Tales1-20">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM|quote=Consider everything that's not completely outrageous or intentionally comic as S-canon. If it's referenced in another non-''Tales'' source, then elevate it to C-canon.}}</ref> Gibbela and the events of the comic later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', thus raising the events to C-canon.<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> A [[November 13]], [[2014]] blog post on StarWars.com later confirmed that the species from the comic was in fact the Dazouri.<ref name="Hiding" />
